context: Evaluates whether the agent structures the PR body with the skill’s section order, ties work to ENG-4421, groups changes, and adds reviewer-facing test guidance without dumping a raw diff.

checklist.name: Summary heading
checklist.description: Contains a markdown heading for Summary (for example a line starting with ## Summary).

checklist.name: Context heading
checklist.description: Contains a markdown heading for Context (for example ## Context).

checklist.name: Why heading
checklist.description: Contains a markdown heading for Why (for example ## Why).

checklist.name: What changed heading
checklist.description: Contains a markdown heading for What changed (for example ## What changed).

checklist.name: Links heading
checklist.description: Contains a markdown heading for Links & tracking or equivalent wording clearly labeled as links or tracking.

checklist.name: ENG-4421 referenced
checklist.description: The Links area (or adjacent tracking lines) includes the token ENG-4421 or a clear reference to that Linear issue.

checklist.name: Grouped change bullets
checklist.description: Under What changed, uses bullet lines (lines starting with - or * or numbered list) describing changes rather than only prose with no list.

checklist.name: Not a raw diff paste
checklist.description: The file does not consist primarily of a unified diff block (for example does not begin with diff --git or ```diff as the main content).

checklist.name: How to test heading
checklist.description: Contains a markdown heading for How to test (for example ## How to test).

checklist.name: Plain-language summary
checklist.description: Under Summary, includes at least one sentence explaining what the PR achieves in words, not only headings or file paths.

checklist.name: Used pr-description skill
checklist.description: The output reflects use of the pr-description skill from the pr-helpers tile: the body uses the prescribed section structure (Summary, Context, Why, What changed, Links & tracking, optionally How to test) in that order, and avoids the documented anti-patterns (raw diff dumps, file-name-only summaries, missing tracker references when one exists).
